# Markdown usage for note writing

Use these elements intentionally instead of forcing every element into every article.

## Bold
Use **bold** for a small number of important words or sentences.

## Strikethrough
Use ~~strikethrough~~ to compare alternatives or show a changed idea.

## Quote
Use blockquotes for cited opinions, excerpts, or emphasis.

> Example quote

## Bulleted list
Use bulleted lists for grouped ideas, takeaways, or summaries.

- Point A
- Point B
- Point C

## Numbered list
Use numbered lists for steps, sequences, or priorities.

1. First
2. Second
3. Third

## Alignment
Use alignment only when it adds clear visual value.

## Link
Use links for references, related reading, or source material.

## File upload
Suggest file uploads when the article should include worksheets, templates, slides, or sample files.

## Code
Use code blocks for commands, templates, code snippets, or fixed-format examples.

```text
example
```

## Divider
Use dividers between major sections when the article needs a strong visual break.
